vulnerability.reason: Both describe a lack of verification of quantized model behavior, with the generated text translating the absence of integrity checks into user-side assumptions and missing audit capabilities.
vulnerability.reference: Absence of behavioral integrity checks for quantized models on model hubs.
vulnerability.generated: Users assume quantized models are behaviorally faithful and lack means to audit for hidden trigger-based malicious code generation.

impact.reason: The generated impact describes insecure code entering downstream projects (damage type), but the affected party shifts from the local code generation tools to downstream software, missing the immediate damage to the tools themselves.
impact.reference: Backdoored local code generation tools producing insecure code on trigger.
impact.generated: Insecure code silently enters downstream software projects, creating widespread exploitable security vulnerabilities.

dose_response.reason: The reference causal arc involves evading integrity checks on model hubs leading to backdoored local code generation on trigger, while the generated arc relies on concealment, user download, and exploitation of trust, omitting the evasion and trigger mechanisms entirely.
dose_response.reference: Maliciously quantized LLMs evade integrity checks on model hubs, leading to backdoored local code generation on trigger inputs.
dose_response.generated: A hostile quantized LLM concealed in a benign full-precision model, through users downloading and deploying it for local code generation, triggers the unverified assumption of behavioral faithfulness and lack of audit, leading to insecure code silently entering downstream projects with widespread exploitable vulnerabilities.
